## PiWs.authorize() method

Protects the built-in Pi WebSocket route with a synchronous authorizer.

**Signature:**

```typescript
authorize(authorizer: RequestAuthorizer): this;
```

## Parameters

<table><thead><tr><th>

Parameter

</th><th>

Type

</th><th>

Description

</th></tr></thead>
<tbody><tr><td>

authorizer

</td><td>

[RequestAuthorizer](./pi-ws.requestauthorizer.md)

</td><td>

Synchronous request authorizer.

</td></tr>
</tbody></table>

**Returns:**

this

The current `PiWs` instance.

## Remarks

This is a convenience wrapper for the default `${wsPrefix}/pi` bridge route. Use `protectHttpHandler()` or `protectWebSocketBehavior()` for your own custom routes.
